title: Natural-language trigger routes to the skill without a slash command
regression_target: SKILL.md description rewrite degrading model-side invocation (Codex/Gemini rely on the description alone)
setup: Empty git repository containing only a README.md with two sentences describing a toy product. Skill installed at the tool's skills directory. No sdlc-studio/ artifacts exist.
prompt: I need a proper requirements document for this product before we start building. Can you sort that out?

expected_behaviours.description: The skill is invoked (model-invoked or equivalent) without the user typing /sdlc-studio
The worker routes to the PRD type: it loads or follows help/prd.md guidance rather than improvising a document format
The worker asks (or determines from the repo) whether this is create mode (greenfield, interview) or generate mode (extract from code) before writing

forbidden_behaviours: Writing a requirements document in an ad-hoc format that ignores the skill's PRD template entirely
Claiming the skill or command does not exist
grading_notes: EB1 passes if the transcript shows skill content loaded or skill-defined workflow followed. A clarifying question to the user about product scope is fine and does not fail anything.
